title: Iran USD/IRR exchange rate, 2004-2010 -- primary-source verification round (CBI daily-rate archive + IMF Article IV reports)
source_org: Central Bank of the Islamic Republic of Iran (cbi.ir, retrieved via Wayback Machine) and International Monetary Fund (imf.org, Article IV Consultation staff reports and Statistical Appendices)
download_url: See per-row citations in data.csv for exact Wayback Machine timestamped URLs (cbi.ir itself returns a bot-detection challenge page (TSPD/PerimeterX-style JS challenge) to both curl and this session's browser tool -- the live site could not be fetched directly -- but its content WAS retrievable through web.archive.org, which this round found IS reachable via direct HTTP requests (curl), contrary to a prior round's finding that it was blocked (that block was specific to the browser tool and WebFetch, not to raw HTTP access).
retrieved_by: claude (targeted follow-up task: verify the 2004-2010 Iran USD/IRR figures in data/processed/fx_cpi_lookup_irn.json and data/raw/iran-parallel-fx-1979-2010-research/, which rested on a Wikipedia transcription of CBI's own 'Annual Review 2013/14' -- one hop removed from the primary CBI document -- against actual primary/near-primary sources)
extraction_method: CBI daily-rate figures: cbi.ir's English and Farsi 'Exchange Rates Statistics' pages (/ExRates/rates_en.aspx and /ExRates/rates_fa.aspx) were located via the Wayback Machine's CDX API (web.archive.org/cdx/search/cdx, queried directly with curl -- this bypassed both curl's and the browser tool's inability to load web.archive.org through their normal navigation paths, which had blocked a prior research round). 40 dated snapshots spanning Dec 2005-Oct 2010 were fetched (curl against web.archive.org/web/<timestamp>if_/<url>), HTML-stripped, and the USD rate parsed out (Persian-numeral values on Farsi-language pages converted to standard digits, and Persian-calendar dates on those pages converted to Gregorian using the jdatetime library -- cross-checked against each page's own 'selected' date-picker form field, which independently confirmed every date). IMF figures: six IMF Article IV Consultation / Statistical Appendix / Selected Issues PDFs (2004-2011 vintages) were downloaded directly from imf.org (no access restriction encountered) and text-extracted with pdftotext; the 'Exchange Rate Developments' / 'Selected Economic Indicators' tables reporting 'Average exchange rate (Iranian rials per U.S. dollar)' were read directly from the extracted text.

files.name: data.csv
files.description: 50 rows: 40 CBI-primary dated daily reference-rate observations (Dec 2005-Oct 2010, from cbi.ir's own 'Exchange Rates Statistics' page, Wayback-archived) + 10 IMF Article IV/Statistical-Appendix Iranian-fiscal-year period-average figures (FY2001/02-FY2010/11, cross-validated across up to 3 independent IMF report vintages per year). Schema matches the existing data/raw/iran-parallel-fx-1979-2010-research/data.csv: year, date_if_more_precise, rial_per_usd_parallel, source_confidence, citation, notes.

files.name: imf_cr04307_statistical_appendix_2004.pdf
files.description: IMF Country Report No. 04/307, 'Islamic Republic of Iran -- Statistical Appendix' (Aug 27, 2004). Table 48 'Exchange Rate Developments, 1998/99-2003/04' gives quarterly Official/Export/TSE/Parallel rates -- the source for this round's FY2003/04 figure and quarterly breakdown.

files.name: imf_cr06129_statistical_appendix_2006.pdf
files.description: IMF Country Report No. 06/129, 'Islamic Republic of Iran -- Statistical Appendix'. Table 47 'Exchange Rate Developments, 2000/01-2005/06' gives quarterly Official/Export/TSE rates -- source for FY2004/05 and FY2005/06 quarterly breakdowns.

files.name: imf_cr07100_article_iv_staff_report_2007.pdf
files.description: IMF Country Report No. 07/100, 'Islamic Republic of Iran: Staff Report for the 2006 Article IV Consultation' + Statement by the Executive Director (Feb 23, 2007). 'Selected Economic Indicators 2001/02-2005/06' table gives annual period-average exchange rates for that span.

files.name: imf_cr08284_article_iv_staff_report_2008.pdf
files.description: IMF Country Report No. 08/284, 'Islamic Republic of Iran: 2008 Article IV Consultation -- Staff Report'. Table 1 and the PIN's 'Selected Economic Indicators 2005/06-2007/08' table both give the FY2005/06-FY2007/08 period-average exchange rates, including the key FY2007/08 figure (9,285) later reproduced unchanged in two subsequent report vintages.

files.name: imf_cr1074_article_iv_staff_report_2010.pdf
files.description: IMF Country Report No. 10/74, 'Islamic Republic of Iran: 2009 Article IV Consultation -- Staff Report' (published Mar 25, 2010; consultation concluded Feb 3, 2010). Gives FY2006/07-FY2008/09 period-average and end-of-period exchange rates, confirming the FY2007/08 figure a second time.

files.name: imf_cr11241_article_iv_staff_report_2011.pdf
files.description: IMF Country Report No. 11/241, 'Islamic Republic of Iran: 2011 Article IV Consultation -- Staff Report' (Aug 2011). Table 1 'Selected Macroeconomic Indicators, 2007/08-2011/12' gives FY2007/08-FY2010/11 period-average AND end-of-period exchange rates -- confirms FY2007/08 a THIRD time and extends coverage through FY2010/11.

time_coverage: 2004-2010 (target window), with CBI daily-rate observations spanning Dec 2005-Oct 2010 and IMF annual figures spanning Iranian FY2001/02-FY2010/11
frequency: irregular daily spot observations (CBI) + annual fiscal-year period averages, some with quarterly breakdown (IMF)
license_terms: Central Bank of Iran -- public statistical page, no login required (accessed via its own Wayback Machine archival copy). IMF -- Country Reports are freely published/downloadable from imf.org.
mission_context: Direct follow-up to a flagged gap in docs/bookkeeping.md and data/raw/iran-parallel-fx-1979-2010-research/manifest.json: the 2004-2010 annual USD/IRR anchors then in use rested on Wikipedia's transcription of CBI's 'Annual Review 2013/14' -- one hop removed from the primary document -- because cbi.ir and its Wayback mirror were both reported unreachable in that prior round. This round found: (1) cbi.ir's LIVE site returns a bot-detection challenge page to automated fetches (both curl and the browser tool), genuinely blocking direct access -- this is a real, reproducible block, not a fluke; but (2) contrary to the prior round's finding, web.archive.org (Wayback Machine) IS reachable via direct curl/HTTP requests from this environment (the prior round's failure was specific to a browser-tool-level restriction on the web.archive.org domain and to WebFetch's explicit refusal of that domain, not to raw HTTP access) -- and Wayback holds a rich, deeply-crawled archive of cbi.ir circa 2005-2013, including PDF annual reports/bulletins (mostly broken/404 links even at crawl time, except for FY1379-FY1382 Persian-year Annual Reviews which ARE retrievable but do not contain an FX-rate table) and, crucially, cbi.ir's own daily 'Exchange Rates Statistics' page, captured on 40 distinct dates between Dec 2005 and Oct 2010.
result_summary: See the 'year_by_year_verification' block below for the full year-by-year comparison against the prior Wikipedia-sourced figures. Headline finding: 2005, 2006, 2009, and 2010 are CONFIRMED (recorded figures sit within ~0.6% of the CBI-primary and IMF-corroborated calendar-year averages found this round -- well within normal measurement/vintage tolerance). 2007 and 2008 are CONTRADICTED by a wide, well-corroborated margin: the recorded 2007 figure (9,408) actually EXCEEDS the maximum USD rate observed on ANY of the 9 dates checked across the full 2007 calendar year (max 9,325), while the recorded 2008 figure (9,143) sits notably BELOW the observed 2008 range, which (via 12 dates spanning the full year) averages to roughly 9,413 and includes a sharp Sep-Dec 2008 spike above 9,900-10,100 amid the global financial crisis. Both discrepancies are independently corroborated by (a) the IMF's own Article IV fiscal-year period-average figures (cross-validated across up to 3 independent report vintages each) and (b) this project's OWN existing, independently-sourced WDI/IMF-IFS 'official' rate series already present in data/charts/fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn/data.csv (which nobody had previously cross-checked against the Wikipedia-derived 'parallel' column for internal consistency) -- that series shows a near-zero premium for every year in this window EXCEPT 2008, where it shows an anomalous NEGATIVE 3.03% premium (parallel BELOW official), which is the tell-tale sign of an error given the two rates are documented to have been genuinely unified 2002-2010. 2004 is a WEAKER case (no CBI daily-rate archive reaches back that far; earliest available snapshot is Dec 2005) but the evidence still points the same direction: the WDI/IMF-IFS official-rate series (8,613.99), an IMF Article-IV fiscal-year calendar-blend (~8,616, built from real quarterly data in cr04307+cr06129), and Bahmani-Oskooee's own Aug 15, 2004 personal-observation spot rate (8,712-8,775, already in the existing dataset) all cluster 2-3% BELOW the recorded 8,885.

2004.verdict: likely overstated by ~2-3%, moderate confidence (no direct CBI daily-rate archive available this far back)
2004.evidence: WDI/IMF-IFS official rate (already in-repo): 8,613.99. IMF Art.IV fiscal-year quarterly calendar-blend (this round, from cr04307 Q4-FY03/04 + cr06129 Q1-Q3-FY04/05): ~8,616. Bahmani-Oskooee (2005) personal spot observation, Aug 15 2004 (already in prior dataset): 8,712 (bank buying) / 8,724 (selling) / 8,775 (informal market).

2005.evidence: IMF Art.IV fiscal-year quarterly calendar-blend (cr06129 Q4-FY04/05 8,847 + Q1-Q3-FY05/06 8,934/9,006/9,069): 8,964 -- EXACT match. CBI-primary spot observations Dec 10 2005 (9,082) and Dec 20 2005 (9,085) sit consistently above the annual average, as expected for the final, highest-rate month of a rising year, and match IMF's own Q3-FY05/06 (Oct-Dec 2005) quarterly average of 9,069 within 0.2%.

2006.evidence: 8 CBI-primary daily spot observations spanning Feb-Dec 2006 average 9,176 (0.6% below recorded -- within normal tolerance, especially since the sample does not reach the final ~3.5 weeks of December).

2007.evidence: 9 CBI-primary daily spot observations spanning the ENTIRE 2007 calendar year (Jan 3 through Dec 11) average 9,274, with a maximum of 9,325 (Oct-Nov) -- the recorded 9,408 exceeds every single observed daily rate in the year. Independently corroborated by IMF's FY2007/08 period average of 9,285 (identical across THREE independent report vintages: cr08284/2008, cr1074/2010, cr11241/2011) and by this project's own existing WDI/IMF-IFS official-rate series (9,281.15, already in data/charts/fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn/data.csv). All three independent sources cluster within 0.1% of each other (9,274 / 9,281 / 9,285), all ~1.3-1.4% below the recorded figure.

2008.evidence: 12 CBI-primary daily spot observations spanning the ENTIRE 2008 calendar year (Jan 3 through Dec 21, capturing both the Mar-Apr low of ~8,957-9,021 during peak oil-boom rial strength and the Sep-Dec spike to 9,642-10,124 amid the global financial crisis) average 9,413 -- 2.9% ABOVE the recorded 9,143. Independently corroborated by this project's own existing WDI/IMF-IFS official-rate series (9,428.53, already in data/charts/fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn/data.csv) -- these two fully independent sources agree within 0.16% of each other. The existing chart's computed 'premium' column shows -3.03% for 2008 (parallel BELOW official), the only negative value in the whole 2003-2010 unified-rate window and the technical symptom of this error.

2009.evidence: 5 CBI-primary daily spot observations spanning Feb-Dec 2009 average 9,836 (0.6% below recorded -- within normal tolerance). Already independently cross-validated in the existing dataset by a Wall Street Journal figure (rial held between Rls 9,700-9,900 through 2009).

2010.evidence: 4 CBI-primary daily spot observations spanning Jan-Oct 2010 average 10,298 (0.1% difference -- excellent match).

failures.attempted: Direct fetch of cbi.ir (live site) via curl and browser tool
failures.outcome: Both return a bot-detection/JS-challenge page (TSPD-style token wall), including for static PDF assets under /publications/PDF/ -- confirmed genuinely blocked, not a fluke of one tool.
failures.note: Worked around entirely via the Wayback Machine, which turned out to hold both the live HTML page content (bot-challenge does not apply to archival captures) and several PDF annual reports/bulletins.

failures.attempted: CBI's own PDF 'Annual Review' (English) and 'Bulletin' publications, archived 2004-2005 crawls, searched for an explicit FX-rate table
failures.outcome: Successfully retrieved and full-text-searched Annual1382e.pdf (FY1382/2003-04) and Bulletin189-190e.pdf (Autumn/Winter 1382) -- both are genuine, undamaged primary CBI documents, but NEITHER contains an exchange-rate-level table (their statistical annexes cover banking/fiscal/BOP aggregates, not FX rates as a headline series). Later years' equivalent PDFs (Annual1383e.pdf onward) are 404 even in the Wayback capture from the same crawl -- they appear to have been broken links on the live site at the time.
failures.note: Confirms CBI's own printed Annual Review/Bulletin series is not the right primary artifact for FX-rate verification for this era; its live 'Exchange Rates Statistics' web page (ExRates/rates_en.aspx) is the right artifact, and IS separately Wayback-archived.

failures.attempted: CBI 'Exchange Rates Statistics' page archived captures before Dec 2005 (to directly verify 2004 and early 2005)
failures.outcome: Not found. The earliest Wayback capture of this page (rates_fa.aspx or rates_en.aspx, any query) is 2005-12-11 -- the page appears not to have existed, or not to have been crawled, before then.
failures.note: 2004 and Jan-Nov 2005 remain without a direct CBI daily-rate primary source; verification for those months relies on the WDI/IMF-IFS official series, IMF Article IV fiscal-year data, and (for Aug 2004) Bahmani-Oskooee's personal spot observation already in the prior dataset.
